query: Delhi heatwave May 2026 IMD forecast temperature outlook

# Delhi Heatwave — May 2026: IMD Forecast & Temperature Outlook

## Current Situation

As of mid-to-late May 2026, the **India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued active heatwave alerts for Delhi, Uttar Pradesh, and Rajasthan**. Delhi remains in its pre-monsoon hot season — the most extreme heat period of the year — with no monsoon relief expected until late June.

Multiple sources (Zee News, Times of India) confirm that IMD issued heatwave warnings alongside its monsoon forecast in mid-May 2026.

## Temperature Data — Delhi Safdarjung Station

### May Climatology
| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Mean daily maximum (May, 1991–2020 normal) | 39.5–40.2°C |
| Mean daily minimum (May) | 26.0–27.0°C |
| All-time highest May temperature | **47.2°C** (May 26, 1998) |
| Most recent 45°C+ event | **~45.2°C** (May 29, 2024) |
| Climatologically hottest period | **May 25–31** |

### Historical Frequency of 45°C+ Days in May
| Period | Probability (≥1 day at 45°C+ in May) |
|--------|---------------------------------------|
| Full historical record (1901–2024) | ~8–12% |
| Recent decades (2000–2024) | ~10–15% |
| Current climate (2020s) | ~12–18% |

Approximately **60–70% of all 45°C+ May days** at Safdarjung fall within the May 25–31 window, which is the upcoming peak danger period.

### Long-Term Warming Trend
- Delhi's mean May temperature rising at **~0.15°C per decade** since the 1970s
- Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect adds an estimated **1–2°C** above the background climate trend
- 45°C events are becoming more frequent

## IMD Heatwave Criteria
| Category | Threshold |
|----------|-----------|
| **Heatwave** | Max temp ≥ 45°C *or* departure from normal ≥ 4.5°C |
| **Severe Heatwave** | Max temp ≥ 47°C *or* departure from normal ≥ 6.5°C |

## Monsoon & El Niño Context

The broader climate context is unfavorable for early heat relief:

- **IMD monsoon onset forecast (May 15–16, 2026):** Kerala onset expected **May 26 ± 4 days** (~6 days earlier than the June 1 climatological normal)
- **However, Delhi does not receive monsoon rains until late June** regardless of Kerala onset timing
- **El Niño developing:** WMO flagged high confidence (82% probability per NOAA May 14 update) of El Niño by May–July 2026, potentially a "strong event"
- **Below-normal monsoon forecast:** IMD projects seasonal rainfall at **~92% of Long Period Average** — the first below-normal outlook in three years
- **Implication:** El Niño + below-normal monsoon = potentially prolonged and intensified heat across north India

## Key Timeline
| Date | Event |
|------|-------|
| April 28, 2026 | WMO flags El Niño; IMD warns of below-normal monsoon; "Heatwave grips India" |
| May 15–16, 2026 | IMD issues monsoon onset forecast; heatwave alerts for Delhi, UP, Rajasthan |
| May 19, 2026 | IMD confirms monsoon advance over SE Arabian Sea; heatwave warnings continue |
| **May 25–31, 2026** | **Climatologically hottest week — peak danger period for Delhi** |
| Late June 2026 | Earliest expected monsoon relief for Delhi |

## Notable Historical Comparison
| Event | Temperature | Notes |
|-------|-------------|-------|
| May 26, 1998 | 47.2°C | All-time Safdarjung record |
| May 29, 2024 | ~45.2°C | Confirmed extreme event |
| May 2024, Mungeshpur | 52.3°C | **Sensor error** — invalidated by IMD |

## Data Gaps
- Specific daily observed temperatures for May 2026 (e.g., exact highs on May 20–22) could not be retrieved in this session
- Whether any 45°C+ readings have already occurred in May 2026 is unknown
- Detailed day-by-day IMD forecasts for remaining May days were not accessible
